@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz models fitted with the OM601–608

mainEntity.name: How much does an OM601–608 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned OM60x engine typically costs £2,000–£3,500 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by cylinder count (I4, I5, I6) and turbo vs naturally aspirated. Fitting adds £550–£1,100 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the OM601–608 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM60x series are legendary for durability, earning 4.5★ out of 5 [ESTIMATED] — million‑mile examples exist. Regular oil changes and injector pump maintenance are the key to longevity.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common OM601–608 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mechanical injection pump wear and glow plug failure are the most common issues — difficult starting, rough idle, and reduced power typically appear after 150,000–200,000 miles. Rebuild kits are available.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the OM601–608?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM60x family spans 190D (W201), E‑Class (W124/W210), S‑Class (W126/W140), G‑Class, Sprinter, and more — truly the backbone of Mercedes diesel production from 1980 to 2001.

mainEntity.name: How long does the OM601–608 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance, 300,000–500,000 miles is common, and many exceed 1,000,000 miles. The engines are overbuilt; the rest of the car usually fails first. Regular injector pump servicing is essential.

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz OM601-OM608 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The OM601–608 family are the legendary over-engineered Mercedes diesel fours, fives and sixes — renowned for million‑mile durability, though injection pump and glow plug issues are well known. Backed by real UK enquiries [ESTIMATED].

verdictRating.confidence: 90% confidence — based on decades of owner feedback, specialist workshop consensus, and extensive forum data; proprietary enquiry figures are estimated.

rows.score: 16 (Most repairs are straightforward; injection pumps and glow plugs are well understood, but some parts (injection pumps) are expensive)
16 (Widely available aftermarket and genuine NOS, but some specific items are getting scarce)
TBC (Depends on host vehicle value — classic/collector values may justify, but everyday examples may approach scrap threshold)
TBC/100 (Sum of the five rows above)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The OM60x diesels are among the most durable engines ever built — but ageing injection pumps and glow plug systems need care — buy well‑maintained examples with documented service history.
verdictRating.bestFor: Classic Mercedes enthusiasts, high‑mileage drivers, and anyone seeking legendary diesel longevity.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You want modern refinement, low emissions, or don't have access to a specialist familiar with mechanical injection.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Cross-link: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an OM60x engine typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED]. Injection pump calibration is a specialist job.
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand cost comparison applies.
